name: Spicy Stuffed Sweet Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 2 large bell peppers (1 red, 1 yellow or orange)
1/8 teaspoon ground cumin, more to taste
2 tablespoons freshly squeezed lime juice, more to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a large baking pan with aluminum foil.
2. Prick the sweet potatoes and place them in the prepared pan. Bake for about 1½ hours or until soft in the center. Remove from the oven, but keep the oven on.
3. Meanwhile, seed and dice the peppers. Cut the potatoes lengthwise to create halves or make slits down the center without cutting through the skin; this helps retain their shape. Scoop out the potato flesh, either by whole or half. Place the empty jackets back onto the baking sheet.
4. Using a fork, mash the potato flesh with butter, cumin, and salt to taste. Spread the mashed mixture evenly over the potato jackets. Sprinkle the stuffed potatoes with cheese and peppers, reserving some peppers for garnish. Return them to the oven until the cheese melts.
5. In a separate bowl, mix the sour cream with lime juice. Garnish the hot stuffed sweet potatoes with remaining peppers, onions, fresh coriander, and the lime-flavored sour cream.
Wine Suggestions
This recipe primarily calls for Chardonnay, but due to the high presence of lime and fresh coriander, opt for a lighter wine rather than one that is full-bodied and woody. Consider trying Chablis A.O.C. or Corine Perchaud.
Per serving: 550 CAL; 15 G PROT; 37 G TOTAL FAT (22 SAT. FAT); 42 G CARB.; 100 MG CHOL; 350 MG SOD.; 6 G FIBER.
